articles.title: U.S.–Iran Conflict Escalates After Two Weeks of Fighting
articles.description: Tensions in the Middle East remain high two weeks after the United States launched major military strikes against Iran, targeting key military infrastructure linked to oil exports. Iran responded with missile and drone attacks, threatening international shipping routes including the Strait of Hormuz. Energy markets surged amid fears of prolonged conflict disrupting global supply chains.

articles.title: Travelers Stranded Abroad as War Disrupts Air Travel
articles.description: International travel has been heavily affected by the escalating conflict. Airlines in Europe, Asia, and the Gulf rerouted flights to avoid conflict zones, leaving several travelers stranded, including newlyweds in the Maldives.

articles.title: Michigan Synagogue Attack Sparks Security Alarm
articles.description: Authorities investigate a violent incident at Temple Israel synagogue where a man rammed a truck into the building before opening fire. Over 100 children were inside at the time, but emergency responders limited casualties. Experts warn ongoing concerns about safety at places of worship.

articles.title: Georgia Community Grieves Teacher’s Death
articles.description: A community in Georgia mourns a beloved teacher who dedicated life to helping others cope with grief. Investigations continue into students believed to be involved, prompting calls for compassion and accountability.

articles.title: Discovery of 150 Dead Sea Turtles Raises Alarm
articles.description: Marine scientists warn after discovering nearly 150 dead sea turtles along a coastal region, likely due to pollution, fishing practices, or climate-driven changes. Conservationists say mass wildlife deaths signal deeper environmental threats.

articles.title: Italian Citizenship Ruling Affects Millions Worldwide
articles.description: A new court interpretation of Italian citizenship laws could restrict eligibility for descendants of Italians abroad, especially in North and South America, creating uncertainty for thousands of pending applications.

articles.title: Prince Harry Book Sparks Controversy
articles.description: A new book about Prince Harry has triggered controversy after the royal spokesperson dismissed claims as a deranged conspiracy. Allegations include royal family tensions already drawing strong media reactions.

articles.title: Harry Styles Redefines Men’s Fashion
articles.description: Pop star Harry Styles challenges fashion norms by appearing with designer handbags traditionally marketed toward women, reflecting growing acceptance of gender-fluid fashion globally.

articles.title: Oscar-Nominated Films Return in Special Screenings
articles.description: Several Academy Awards nominated films return to theaters with immersive screenings and extended versions, allowing audiences closer experiences with the filmmaking process.

articles.title: CKay Pushes Boundaries of Afro-Fusion
articles.description: Nigerian artist CKay plans new projects exploring 'Emo-Afrobeats', blending Afrobeats with emotional pop sounds, aiming to expand African music’s global influence.

articles.title: United States Advances in World Baseball Classic
articles.description: The United States defeated Canada 5–3 to reach the semifinals of the World Baseball Classic, maintaining momentum for a strong tournament finish.

articles.title: Security Concerns Delay Players Championship Opening
articles.description: Security officials delayed entry gates at the Players Championship golf tournament due to a nearby manhunt related to a fatal shooting. No direct threat to the venue was found.

articles.title: Four Astronauts Cleared for Moon Mission
articles.description: NASA approved the launch of four astronauts for an upcoming Moon mission, marking another step in renewed long-term lunar exploration and future Mars missions.

articles.title: Nigerian Student Dies After Plasma Donation in Canada
articles.description: A 22-year-old Nigerian student reportedly died in Canada following complications linked to plasma donation procedures, prompting calls for stricter monitoring of private clinics.

articles.title: Lagos Resumes Monthly Environmental Sanitation
articles.description: Authorities in Lagos announced resumption of monthly sanitation exercises starting April 25 to improve public health and waste management.

articles.title: Nigerian Football Legend Henry Nwosu Dies at 62
articles.description: Former Nigeria national football team midfielder Henry Nwosu passed away at 62. Nwosu was a pioneer of modern Nigerian football and helped the country rise on the African stage.

articles.title: Universities Face Tension Over Salaries
articles.description: Academic Staff Union at Benue State University threatens strike over unpaid salaries and pensions. University of Ilorin partners with Nigerian National Petroleum Company to establish energy research center and professorial chair.

articles.title: Disney Parks Transformation Continues
articles.description: The Walt Disney Company introduces major upgrades in theme parks including advanced attractions, digital experiences, and new entertainment offerings under new leadership.

articles.title: Can Attention Spans Be Repaired?
articles.description: A 30-day digital detox experiment exploring ways to rebuild focus in the age of smartphones has gained attention among psychologists studying technology's impact on attention and mental well-being.
